paper_title: A high resolution melting method for the molecular identification of the potentially toxic diatom Pseudo-nitzschia spp. in the Mediterranean Sea

methods.description: Process for analyzing raw melting curve data using High Resolution Melt Software to identify Pseudo-nitzschia species based on melting temperature and curve shape.

steps.description: The raw melting curve data were processed by the High Resolution Melt Software v. 3.0.1 (Applied Biosystems, Foster City, CA, USA).
steps.searchable_summary: process raw melting curve data using HRM Software v. 3.0.1

steps.description: The pre-and post-melt regions were set as close as possible to the melting transition region.
steps.searchable_summary: set pre-and post-melt regions near melting transition

steps.description: Positive controls, one for each species tested, were set in the HRM software assigning each control to its corresponding well.
steps.searchable_summary: assign positive controls to corresponding wells in HRM software

steps.description: Samples were analyzed and the software automatically made a call for each sample according to the shape of the melt curves aligned to the controls and the melting temperature (T m ).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze samples and make automatic calls based on melt curves and Tm

methods.description: Validation and application of the HRM assay for identifying Pseudo-nitzschia species using DNA samples from known and unknown strains.

steps.description: The method was validated using several DNAs (n = 22) of Pseudo-nitzschia, already used as controls.
steps.searchable_summary: validate HRM assay using known DNA samples

steps.description: The method was subsequently applied to 29 strains of unidentified Pseudo-nitzschia spp. isolated from seawater off the coast (northwestern Adriatic Sea) between November 2014 and March 2015.
steps.searchable_summary: apply HRM assay to unidentified Pseudo-nitzschia strains
